ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., temperature, pressure, vibration) */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment the sensor is installed on */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "sensor_model" TEXT /* Specific model of the sensor */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "sensor_status" TEXT /* Current status of the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "equipment" (
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each equipment */,
  "equipment_name" TEXT /* Name of the equipment */,
  "production_line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line the equipment belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the equipment */,
  "equipment_model" TEXT /* Specific model of the equipment */,
  "equipment_status" TEXT /* Current status of the equipment */,
  "maintainer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for maintaining the equipment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("equipment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_equipment_production_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("production_line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("production_line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_lines" (
  "production_line_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production line */,
  "production_line_name" TEXT /* Name of the production line */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the production line */,
  "production_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum production capacity of the line */,
  "production_line_status" TEXT /* Current status of the production line */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("production_line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that took the reading */,
  "reading_date" TEXT /* Date the reading was taken */,
  "reading_type" TEXT /* Type of reading taken (e.g., temperature, pressure, vibration) */,
  "reading_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the reading */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data collected */,
  "pt1x" REAL /* X-coordinate of point 1 */,
  "pt1y" REAL /* Y-coordinate of point 1 */,
  "pt2x" REAL /* X-coordinate of point 2 */,
  "pt2y" REAL /* Y-coordinate of point 2 */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., maintenance, quality control, administrator) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance activity */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment maintained */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was performed */,
  "maintenance_description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the data */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor whose data was accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_logs_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment that triggered the alert */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., equipment failure, sensor malfunction) */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)
